ownerBio: Peter Bricks is the founder and sole attorney at Peter Bricks, PC (Bricks Law). He holds a Bachelor of Journalism from the University of Texas at Austin (1998) and a Juris Doctor from Georgia State University (2006). He has been an active member in good standing of the State Bar of Georgia since 2006, admitted before the Supreme Court of Georgia, the Georgia Court of Appeals, and the U.S. District Courts for the Northern and Middle Districts of Georgia. He is a registered civil mediator with the Georgia Office of Dispute Resolution and a member of the National Association of Consumer Bankruptcy Attorneys and the American Bankruptcy Institute. Clients consistently describe him as responsive, thorough, and willing to dig deeper into a case than a prior attorney did, most notably in a case where he uncovered over $1 million in additional insurance coverage a previous attorney had missed.
ownerValues: Accessibility, fast response to calls and emails
Thoroughness, explaining the legal process in plain language
Persistence, re-examining cases other attorneys gave up on
Personal attention, clients deal directly with the attorney
missionStatement: Your Trusted Legal Advocate in Metro Atlanta, dedicated to providing compassionate legal assistance.

services: Personal injury, pedestrian and hit-and-run claims

pricing.model: Contingency fee for personal injury cases (no recovery, no fee implied but not stated verbatim); flat-fee/hourly for bankruptcy filings
pricing.termsDescription: Personal injury clients pay nothing unless the firm recovers a settlement or verdict. Bankruptcy filing fees follow standard Chapter 7/13 consumer rates.

brandVoice: Approachable, plain-spoken, client-first and informational rather than aggressive. Unusual for the PI niche, the current site reads more like a helpful neighborhood attorney than a hard-charging injury fighter, even though the underlying case results (a $1.325M recovery after finding coverage a prior attorney missed, a $300K jury verdict won despite a denied-causation defense) support a much stronger, more confident voice.
primaryColorGuess: Not formally documented, current Wix site uses a black wordmark logo on white
tagline: Atlanta Personal Injury and Bankruptcy Attorney, Your Trusted Legal Advocate in Metro Atlanta
techDifferentiators: Active blog with bankruptcy and personal injury legal content
"Lawyers and Lay People" podcast with guest attorneys (landlord-tenant law, criminal law, social security disability, lemon law)
uniqueStoryHooks: Found over $1 million in additional insurance coverage a prior attorney missed, turned a $25,000 settlement into $1,325,000
Won a $300,000 Gwinnett County jury verdict despite the defense denying causation and arguing for $0-$140,000
Secured a $100,000 uninsured-motorist policy-limits recovery in a hit-and-run case by sourcing dashcam evidence via an open-records request after police blamed the client
Solo practitioner, clients deal directly with Peter Bricks rather than a call center or rotating associate
19+ years of Georgia bar standing plus federal court admissions and a GODR mediator credential
techStack: Wix, HTTPS enabled, Organization + LocalBusiness JSON-LD schema present on homepage, responsive by default
currentSiteIssues: Homepage messaging split roughly evenly between personal injury and bankruptcy with no clear hierarchy
No prominent conversion CTA beyond a phone number
Settlement results buried on a secondary page instead of featured on the homepage
Mailing address is a P.O. Box rather than the firm's real office address
No "No Win, No Fee" or 24/7-availability language anywhere on the current site
Likely slow mobile load (Wix, large hero image assets)

contingencyFee.termsText: Represents injured plaintiffs on a contingency basis; "No Win, No Fee" phrasing not currently used on site, recommended as a locked-copy addition

settlementResults.description: Co-counsel for a middle-aged man badly injured in an auto collision (surgical ankle/patella fixation, post-surgical infection). A prior attorney had recovered $25,000; Bricks Law uncovered over $1 million in additional applicable insurance.

Jury verdict in Gwinnett County for a college student with bilateral knee injuries (arthroscopy, partial meniscectomy) from being T-boned by a driver who ran a red light. Verdict secured despite the defense denying causation and arguing for $0-$140,000.

Policy-limits settlement under the client's uninsured motorist policy after she was run off the highway by a hit-and-run driver. Police initially cited the client; Bricks Law obtained dashcam video via an open-records request to prove fault lay with the other driver.

Settlement plus bad-faith claim recovery for a client who required ankle-fracture surgery; only $55,000 in insurance was available, an additional $20,000 was secured above policy limits via a bad-faith claim.
Pedestrian case, client's foot was run over by a vehicle in a collision; settlement amount confidential.
